learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate critical understanding of key concepts - Demonstrate an understanding of key communication theories.
Demonstrate critical understanding of key concepts - Apply communication theories studied to understand everyday communication contexts.
Demonstrate critical understanding of key concepts - Understand the link between communication theory and research.
Apply communication theory to analyse and understand mass media and group phenomena.

assessments.task_description:
The aim of the first assessment is to understand how theory is applied in everyday life and in research. You will choose one of the theories covered in Weeks 2 and 4 and then write a report that includes the following sections: 1) a description of the theory, 2) a description of an experience from your own life and a reflection on how the chosen theory helps to make sense of the experience, 3) a brief literature review showing how the theory is used in research.

The full assessment guidelines and marking rubric will be available on the Course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Theoretical analysis of a communication campaign
assessments.task_description: The aim of this assignment is to help you to use theory to critique real world communication campaigns that seek to influence people. You will choose a communication campaign that has been conducted in Australia. Your task is to analyse the extent to which the campaign illustrates aspects of one of the public communication theories, covered in Weeks 5 to 7. Through the theoretical analysis you will draw conclusions about whether the communication campaign is likely to be successful and make recommendations for how it could have been improved.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The end of semester exam will assess the content from Weeks 9 - 12. It will consist of short answer and essay questions. A practice exam will be made available to students. The exam will be centrally administered and held during the Examination period.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
